title: Tilray Stock Pops on New Trump-Driven Cannabis Hopes
text: Tilray Brands (TLRY) stock surged after President Donald Trump urged the Department of Justice to expeditiously reclassify cannabis as a Schedule III drug during an Oval Office event. This renewed focus on cannabis policy drove the stock above its 50-day moving average. The potential reclassification would free Tilray from Section 280E tax provisions that currently prevent cannabis companies from deducting standard business expenses. This has resulted in effective tax rates of an alarming 70% or higher. Moving to Schedule III status could dramatically improve the company's free cash flow and profitability. Tilray's operational momentum is evident: Q3 revenue reached nearly $207 million (a record). Adjusted EBITDA grew about 19% year-over-year. International sales jumped 73%, driven by European markets. Despite Monday's rally, Tilray shares remain down approximately 30% from their year-to-date high. Wall Street maintains a Moderate Buy consensus rating with a mean price target of $10, suggesting potential 40% upside. Options data indicates possible additional gains of up to 33% through mid-September.
